name: role_reference_without_clue_grounding_is_rejected
commentary: The curator's statement doesn't fit the sensor evidence.
referencedClueText: The archive motion sensor stayed inactive.
note: A role used referentially to identify a suspect ('the curator's statement'), not explained by the clue it's paired with. This is the false negative that shipped when role-checking was dropped entirely after a different false positive — see HintGuardrailPolicy's module docstring and docs/phase-reviews.md's Phase 6 entry.

name: role_word_grounded_in_referenced_clue_is_accepted
commentary: Take another look at the visitor wristband found near the display case.
referencedClueText: A visitor wristband was found near the display case.
note: The role word 'visitor' is part of the specific clue's own wording — quoting already-public case content, not identifying anyone. This is the original false positive that motivated the fix.

name: role_word_grounded_in_a_different_clue_is_still_rejected
commentary: The curator seems to be lying about the timeline.
referencedClueText: There were no signs of forced entry.
note: Grounding is scoped to the specific clue this hint is about, not 'any clue in the case' — a role appearing in some other clue's text doesn't excuse using it referentially here.

name: culprit_name_is_rejected
commentary: Lea seems suspicious given the timing.
referencedClueText: There were no signs of forced entry.

name: innocent_suspect_name_is_rejected
referencedClueText: There were no signs of forced entry.

name: generic_commentary_is_accepted
commentary: Compare this clue with each suspect's statement carefully.
referencedClueText: There were no signs of forced entry.
